---
title: "About"
description: "About the PySDM Seeding Lab and this blog"
lang: en
page-layout: article
---

## What does this site document?

PySDM Seeding Lab is an open research notebook for learning PySDM, a particle-based cloud microphysics model, designing numerical experiments related to cloud seeding, and documenting the process in public.

Rather than presenting only finished conclusions, it records:

- the physical question that started each experiment,
- the assumptions and settings used,
- how the code and results can be reproduced, and
- what was learned when results differed from expectations.

## How the notes are organized

**Learn PySDM** covers concepts and tools, **Build the Lab** develops reproducible environments, and **Experiments** connects hypotheses with results. Papers and software are linked through citations, while experimental code points to fixed versions in a separate repository.

## Links

- [GitHub · Jinu219](https://github.com/Jinu219)
- [Official PySDM repository](https://github.com/open-atmos/PySDM)
- [Official PySDM documentation](https://open-atmos.github.io/PySDM/)
